approval.note: Publish only small source-reviewed batches. Do not trade catalogue size for weaker Commons evidence, stale links, private locations or synthetic geometry.

metadata.scheduling_boundary: follow-up after real editorial use and source review

title: Grow the trust-first Commonworld catalogue from 30 toward 40–50 Commons
goal: Add further small, source-backed catalogue batches that reduce remaining regional and domain gaps while preserving privacy, action-link evidence, freshness dates and one canonical CommonProject identity per Commons.

acceptance.assertion: Each publication batch is independently reviewable, source-checked and bound to an exact commit and public readback.
Selection is driven by measured regional and domain gaps, including additional southern-hemisphere, care, water, housing, repair and local-network cases where primary-near evidence exists.
Every new identity has official or primary-near sources, real action links, an explicit review date and a next-review date.
No private site, household, router, roof, clinic, plot or infrastructure path is reconstructed, and public geometry requires a separately justified source.
Coverage contracts use minimums and diversity properties rather than a fixed catalogue-size ceiling.
